input.background: An investor in wheat futures is concerned with the events related to U.S. wheat production profitability and the occurrence of a serious drought. The investor has the following probabilities: P(B | A) = 0.01 (the probability that wheat production will be profitable given a serious drought occurs) and P(A) = 0.05 (the probability of a serious drought occurring).
input.question: What is the probability that a serious drought will occur and that a profit will be made? That is, find P(A ∩ B), the probability of the intersection of events A and B.

output.answer: We want to calculate P(A ∩ B). Using the formula for the multiplicative rule, we obtain P(A ∩ B) = P(A) * P(B | A) = (0.05)(0.01) = 0.0005. The probability that a serious drought occurs and the production of wheat is profitable is only 0.0005. As we might expect, this intersection is a very rare event.

meta_info.caption: Applying the Multiplicative Rule-Wheat Futures
meta_info.section: 3.6 The Multiplicative Rule and Independent Events
